preamble: The spirantization of a palatalized bilabial stop [pʲ] into a bilabial fricative [ɸ] is a key stage in the historical development of the Japonic consonant system.

examples.note: The shift from p > ɸ > h in Japanese history. Palatalized variants followed this trajectory during the development of Modern Japanese.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
